title: Goal Contracts and Observable Completion · Guided Harness Build
summary: Turn a vague long-running objective into an executable goal contract. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a measurable goal and done-condition specification while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Write a goal as an outcome rather than an unbounded activity.
Attach observable success measures, boundaries, and evidence requirements.
Separate current objective, durable constraints, and optional next work.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

stimulus.title: Goal Hierarchy failure evidence

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Goal Hierarchy condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Goal Hierarchy applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Relate mission, milestone, task, and step without losing the terminal outcome.
